skill.name: firecrawl-search
skill.description: Web search with full page content extraction. Use this skill whenever the user asks to search the web, find articles, research a topic, look something up, find recent news, discover sources, or says 'search for', 'find me', 'look up', 'what are people saying about', or 'find articles about'. Returns real search results with optional full-page markdown — not just snippets. Provides capabilities beyond Claude's built-in WebSearch.
skill.summary: Web search with full page content extraction for AI assistants.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 25 patterns but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation only containing markdown examples and YAML frontmatter. No executable code, shell commands, or filesystem operations exist in this file. Safe to publish.

content.user_title: Search the Web and Extract Full Page Content
content.value_statement: Standard web search tools return only snippets. This skill performs web searches and optionally extracts full page content in markdown format for deeper analysis.
actual_capabilities: Perform web searches with customizable parameters (sources, time range, location)
Extract full page content in markdown format from search results
Filter results by category (GitHub, research papers, PDFs)
Export results as JSON for further processing
Integrate with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows
limitations: Requires firecrawl CLI to be installed and configured with API key
Search results depend on firecrawl service availability and rate limits
Full page scraping consumes additional credits per URL
Cannot execute JavaScript or interact with dynamic web pages

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Find and extract comprehensive information on research topics, automatically gathering full article content for synthesis and analysis.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Search for competitor mentions, extract full blog posts and news articles, and compile structured reports from multiple sources.

use_cases.title: Code Discovery
use_cases.description: Search GitHub repositories and technical documentation, extract full code examples and implementation details for development tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent articles about machine learning best practices and return the top 5 results with titles and URLs.

prompt_templates.title: News Search with Time Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Find news articles about AI regulation from the past 24 hours. Extract full content from each result and summarize the key points.

prompt_templates.title: Research with Full Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for academic papers on transformer architectures. For each result, scrape the full page content and extract the abstract, methodology, and conclusions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep research requiring full document analysis

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Source Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for discussions about the new JavaScript framework across news, GitHub repositories, and technical blogs. Categorize results by source type and extract full content from the top 3 results in each category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive topic investigation across multiple source types

output_examples.input: Search for Python async best practices with full content extraction
output_examples.output: JSON file containing 10 search results with titles, URLs, snippets, and full markdown content from each page ready for analysis

output_examples.input: Find recent news about climate policy with past week filter
output_examples.output: Structured news results showing 15 articles from the past 7 days with source, publication date, headline, and summary for each

best_practices: Always save results to .firecrawl/ directory to avoid context window overflow
Use --scrape flag during initial search rather than re-scraping URLs from results to save credits
Apply specific time filters (qdr:d, qdr:w) when researching time-sensitive topics to improve relevance
anti_patterns: Do not scrape all search results unless needed - use selective extraction to minimize costs
Avoid storing sensitive data or API keys in output files in plain text
Do not use for automated mass scraping that violates target site terms of service

faq.question: What is the difference between firecrawl-search and Claude's built-in WebSearch?
faq.answer: firecrawl-search returns actual search results with optional full page content extraction in markdown format, while WebSearch provides summarized answers. Use firecrawl-search when you need raw results or complete page content for analysis.

faq.question: Do I need a firecrawl API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the firecrawl CLI requires an API key. Install the CLI and configure your key before using this skill.

faq.question: How do I extract specific fields from search results?
faq.answer: Results are saved as JSON. Use tools like jq to extract specific fields: jq -r '.data.web[].url' .firecrawl/search.json extracts all URLs.

faq.question: Can I search only news sources?
faq.answer: Yes, use --sources news along with --tbs qdr:d for past day news or qdr:w for past week results.

faq.question: What file format are results saved in?
faq.answer: Results are saved as JSON files when using the --json flag. The full page content is extracted as markdown when using --scrape.

faq.question: How do I limit the number of search results?
faq.answer: Use the --limit flag followed by a number, for example: --limit 5 returns only the top 5 results.
